name: Coding Assessment IDE & Workflow
description: Bring the coding assessment participant experience in line with the quiz assessment: enforce the same consent/fullscreen/screen-share proctoring workflow, and restyle the coding IDE to resemble a light VS Code environment with a clean, functional terminal/output panel.

goal.answer: Bring the coding assessment participant experience in line with the quiz assessment: enforce the same consent/fullscreen/screen-share proctoring workflow, and restyle the coding IDE to resemble a light VS Code environment with a clean, functional terminal/output panel.

criteria.answer:
Coding assessment uses the same 3-step consent gate as quiz (consent → screen share → fullscreen).
Fullscreen exit enforces the same 3-strike warning/auto-submit behavior used in QuizTaking.
Screen share stop pauses the exam and auto-submits if not resumed within 30 seconds, matching QuizTaking.
CodeEditor is locked to a light/white VS Code-style theme and wrapper; no dark mode toggle.
The run/output panel shows stdout, stderr, compile output, execution status, time/memory, and test results in a clean, IDE-like layout.

constraints.answer: Use only existing dependencies; do not install new packages., Preserve all existing backend API contracts for coding run/submit/save., Keep existing QuizTaking component and quiz flow untouched., CodeEditor must remain light/white only (no dark theme toggle).

phases.answer: Extract shared proctoring shell, Refactor coding attempt page, Light VS Code IDE theme, IDE-style output panel

assumptions.answer: The user wants the light/white theme as the only theme for the coding IDE.; The same proctoring session APIs used by quiz (proctorApi.startSession/activate/violation) apply to coding assessments with assessmentType='coding'.; A reusable proctoring shell can be created without modifying QuizTaking.

phases.name: Extract shared proctoring shell
phases.goal: Create a reusable exam proctoring wrapper that enforces fullscreen, screen-share reconnect, and 3-strike termination so coding assessment can use it.
phases.description: Build a small wrapper component that implements the fullscreen 3-strike rule and screen-share lifecycle (attach track ended, pause overlay, reconnect countdown, auto-submit). It will accept children, onSubmit callback, and screenStream prop.

steps.description: Create src/proctoring/components/ExamProctorShell.jsx with fullscreen 3-strike logic and screen-share pause/resume overlay extracted from QuizTaking.

phases.summary: Created a reusable ExamProctorShell component that replicates QuizTaking's fullscreen 3-strike rule and screen-share pause/resume/auto-submit behavior. The file compiles with the production build.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Refactor coding attempt page
phases.goal: Wire the coding assessment through the same consent gate and proctoring shell as the quiz.
phases.description: Update ParticipantCodingAttemptPage to use the extracted proctoring shell after consent, removing its manual fullscreen toggle and ad-hoc screen-share restart. Keep its code editor and output panel intact.

steps.description: Replace inline fullscreen/screen-share UI in ParticipantCodingAttemptPage with ExamProctorShell, passing the existing submit handler.

phases.summary: ParticipantCodingAttemptPage now routes through AssessmentConsentGate and is wrapped by ExamProctorShell, mirroring the quiz flow. Manual fullscreen toggle and screen-share restart UI were removed.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Light VS Code IDE theme
phases.goal: Make the coding editor look like a light VS Code instance.
phases.description: Update CodeEditor to use a light Monaco theme and style the toolbar/wrapper with a light color palette. Remove the theme toggle so only light theme is available.

steps.description: Set CodeEditor theme to 'light' and update wrapper/toolbar colors to a VS Code light palette; remove theme toggle button.

phases.summary: CodeEditor is locked to a light/white VS Code-style theme and wrapper with no dark-mode toggle. Production build passes.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: IDE-style output panel
phases.goal: Restyle the output/terminal area to look and behave like a real IDE panel.
phases.description: Update the output panel in ParticipantCodingAttemptPage to use light theme tabs, clear section labels, and readable status colors while preserving stdout/stderr/compile/test results display.

steps.description: Restyle output panel with light theme tabs and status badges for stdout/stderr/compile/test results.

Screen share stop pauses the exam and auto-submits if not resumed within 30 seconds, matching QuizTaking.

The run/output panel shows stdout, stderr, compile output, execution status, time/memory, and test results in a clean, IDE-like layout.
